task: Slow down/throttle Brave Search API usage in this Next.js app so we stop hitting HTTP 429 RATE_LIMITED on Brave Free plan. Grounded entrypoints (from initial scan): - web/src/lib/braveSearch.ts (braveWebSearch wrapper, throws on non-OK) - web/src/pages/api/analyze.ts (calls braveWebSearch using BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY) Goal: introduce pacing / concurrency control / retry/backoff / caching so the app stays under Brave's rate limit.
context:
Success criteria (initial guess): - Brave Search calls are rate-limited to <= 1 request/second (or whatever Brave indicates) across relevant code paths. - If Brave returns 429, we back off and retry (bounded) or degrade gracefully without crashing the whole analysis. - Avoid parallel Brave calls that exceed the limit. - Keep code consistent with existing patterns in repo.
Known constraints: - Brave Free plan rate_limit appears to be 1 (from error meta.rate_limit=1). - Project is a Next.js app under web/ using pages/api.
Non-goals / out of scope: - Upgrading Brave plan / external infra changes unless already present. - Major redesign of analysis pipeline.
Token budget target: ~60k
discovery_agent-guidelines: Focus on likely entrypoints + adjacent impacted systems. Prefer codemaps/slices for breadth; full content only for key files. Return: 1) Current-state summary (facts only; cite paths) 2) Existing patterns/conventions worth following (cite paths) 3) Candidate open decisions, tagged FACT vs DESIGN vs PREFERENCE
